headline: South Korea unveils $520 billion investment plan with Samsung and SK Hynix to expand memory chip dominance — plan includes four new fabs and HBM facilities, amid strong government support
why: Flagship-scale: $520B with four named fabs is a massive Investor commitment that will cascade immediately to fab equipment suppliers and construction, with memory oversupply risk arriving 3-5 years out.

headline: US Utilities Increase Spending Plans to $1.5 Trillion Through 2030, Research Report Says
why: Flagship: $1.5T utility capex through 2030 is one of the largest cascade triggers in the economy — data center and electrification demand forcing utilities to build generation and grid, with equipment suppliers (GE, Siemens, ABB) capturing immediate revenue while rate-base recovery and oversupply risks arrive years later.

headline: UK's Starmer unveils $20 billion defence boost in long-delayed investment plan
why: $20B defence capex inflection from a sovereign Investor — government spend cascades immediately to defence suppliers (BAE, Rolls, Babcock) while capacity bottlenecks and cost overruns arrive later.

headline: Renk's Stock Trades Near Trough While Record Order Book Signals a Fundamental Mismatch
why: Defence transmission supplier with record order book but stock near trough — market is pricing the bust (defence spending cuts?) while the supplier is still booking peak revenue; a classic cascade timing mismatch.

headline: Aircraft deliveries hit record high as global demand fuels £388bn UK aerospace pipeline

headline: SAS orders up to 40 Airbus A330s in $10 billion fleet expansion
why: SAS's $10B widebody order is a classic Investor move — airline converts balance sheet into aircraft, Airbus books revenue now while oversupply risk lands on the airline years later.

why: Named base upgrade (Faslane) is a distinct sub-cascade within the UK defence plan — concrete infrastructure capex flowing to construction and naval engineering suppliers, separate from the headline spend figure.

headline: Can Vishay's Massive Capacity Expansion Unlock Margin Growth Ahead?
